title: RE: CALIFORNIA PUBLIC RECORDS ACT REQUEST – SPECIFIC FINANCIAL INSTRUMENT RECEIPT (Santa Monica College)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

To the Respective Coordinators,

Under the California Public Records Act (Gov. Code § 7920.000 et seq.), I hereby request the disclosure of the following records. Given the history of technical denials, I demand a full search for the following:

1. Specific Financial Disposition (SMC District)

Receipt Records: All logs/entries confirming receipt of USPS Tracking #9589071052701840988203 (Delivered Feb 24, 2026) addressed to Robert M. Myers at 1900 Pico Blvd.

Usage/Allocation: If the $0.60 check was deposited, provide the fund allocation and expenditure records.

Rejection Justification: If not deposited, provide the internal memo or policy citing the reason for rejection/non-deposit.

2. Historical Pattern of Administrative Bias (Young Jin Jung)

Denial Logs: A comprehensive list of all prior payments or CPRA requests from "Young Jin Jung" that were rejected.

Nomenclature Precedents: Records showing denials based on minor clerical issues (e.g., "SMCC" vs. full District name) to determine if the District is engaging in pretextual obstruction.

3. Characterization & Framing Logs (UC Berkeley/UCPD)

"Idolization" Rhetoric: Any and all internal communications (emails, Slack, memos) where the term "idolize" or similar framing was used to characterize my inquiries regarding the 2013 SMC shooting.

Retaliatory Reporting: All records of communication with external government/law enforcement agencies regarding my person, including which agency was dispatched and the specific justification/dispatch logs provided.

Victim Status Acknowledgment: Records discussing my status as a victim/survivor of the 2013 shooting and any decision to disregard this status in administrative dealings.

Legal Notice: * Redaction Mandate: Per Gov. Code § 7922.525, you must redact sensitive info and release the remainder; a blanket denial is a violation.

Duty to Assist: Per Gov. Code § 7922.600, you are obligated to assist in narrowing this search if deemed "overbroad."

Deadline: A determination is required within 10 days.

Sincerely,

Young Jin Jung

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Young Jin Jung
